subject: tensor-chain-fmax-n1
notes: kind=estimate per issue #9 text ('fmax per N from timing report, kind: estimate until hardware-run') — this is a Quartus Timing Analyzer static-timing result (Fmax Summary, worst-case setup slack -13.438 ns at the 300 MHz/3.333 ns SDC target), not a silicon measurement; no AXC3000 board available in this session. dsp_tensor_mode=0 is real and expected, not a bug: rtl/microbench/l0_tensor_chain/README.md documents that Quartus Prime Pro 26.1 refuses to elaborate the tensor-mode WYSIWYG primitive for FAMILY "Agilex 3" (message 24863: 'The native AI-optimized DSP (DSP Prime block) only supports Stratix 10 NX and Agilex 5 devices') — this N=1 revision is confirmed CLASSIC-MODE RTL (5 real Fixed-Point [A] DSP blocks, 0 DSP_PRIME [C] blocks), the exact 'silent 10x loss' PLAN §3 LV2 warns about, not the tensor-mode target. scripts/audit_tensor_mode.py correctly FAILs this report when checked against an expected tensor count > 0. Emitted manually (not yet by sw/host/run_l0.py, which needs hardware) from a real `quartus_sh --flow compile l0_tensor_chain -c l0_tensor_chain_n1` run in this session.
